title: Mining Tips
quickAnswer: Use branch mining when you want controlled, low-risk ore access, and use caving when you can safely expose lots of stone or deepslate quickly. The best method depends on the ore and your gear.
keyFacts: Modern diamonds are often found efficiently by deep caving or branch mining in deepslate layers around the high-yield band.
Caving is stronger for exposed ores and faster scouting, while branch mining is steadier and safer in dangerous terrain.
Bring blocks, food, water or bucket utility, and a plan for lava because deep mining mistakes are usually positioning mistakes.
commonMistakes: Using one mining method for every ore instead of adapting by height, biome, and danger.
Treating deep lava layers casually and losing gear to a single exposed pocket.
